lede: A look at June 28 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Tom Bischof and Michael Crafter, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2016 Atatürk Airport attack
events.desc: A terrorist attack in Turkey's Istanbul Atatürk Airport kills 42 people and injures more than 230 others.

events.title: Supreme Court of the United States
events.desc: The United States Supreme Court upholds the constitutionality of the Affordable Care Act's individual mandate in National Federation of Independent Business v. Sebelius.

events.title: Slobodan Milošević
events.desc: Slobodan Milošević is extradited to the ICTY in The Hague to stand trial.

events.title: Korean War
events.desc: Korean War: Suspected communist sympathizers (between 60,000 and 200,000) are executed in the Bodo League massacre.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Nazi Germany starts its strategic summer offensive against the Soviet Union, codenamed Case Blue.

events.title: Nakhla meteorite
events.desc: The Nakhla meteorite, the first one to suggest signs of aqueous processes on Mars, falls to Earth, landing in Egypt.

events.title: SS Norge
events.desc: The SS Norge runs aground on Hasselwood Rock in the North Atlantic 430 kilometres (270 mi) northwest of Ireland. More than 635 people die during the sinking.

events.title: United States Court of Private Land Claims
events.desc: The United States Court of Private Land Claims rules James Reavis's claim to Barony of Arizona is "wholly fictitious and fraudulent."

events.title: American Revolutionary War
events.desc: American Revolutionary War: The Battle of Sullivan's Island ends with the American victory, leading to the commemoration of Carolina Day.

events.title: Edward IV
events.desc: Edward, Earl of March, is crowned King Edward IV of England.

births.name: Mike White (filmmaker)
births.line: American actor, director, producer, and screenwriter

births.name: Alexis Carrel
births.line: French surgeon and biologist, Nobel Prize laureate (died 1944)

deaths.text: Clifford Dupont, English-Rhodesian lawyer and politician, 1st President of Rhodesia
Archduke Franz Ferdinand of Austria, archduke of Austria
Alexandros Rizos Rangavis, Greek poet and politician, Greek Foreign Minister

observances.text: International LGBTQ Pride Day, marking the anniversary of Stonewall riots.
Christian feast day: Irenaeus of Lyon (Western Christianity)
Christian feast day: Blessed Maria Pia Mastena
